title: Replace local AI analysis with external REST API integration

requirements.summary: Remove all local AI analysis logic from ProcessingScreen.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: ProcessingScreen.tsx no longer generates mock analysis data locally
All simulated delay logic for fake AI processing is removed
Frontend depends entirely on external API responses for analysis results

file_operations.description: Remove the mock analysis generation logic that creates simulated AnalysisResult objects with hardcoded values. Delete the setTimeout-based fake processing delays and random value generation for direction, confidence, patterns, and resistance levels. Keep only the processing stage animation UI and prepare to call external API service instead.

requirements.summary: Create API service module for external REST endpoint integration
acceptanceCriteria: API service module exports a function that accepts an image file (File or Blob)
Function constructs multipart/form-data request with 'imagem' field containing the image
Function sends POST request to '/analisar-grafico' endpoint
Function returns a Promise that resolves to the parsed JSON response

file_operations.description: Create a new API service module that exports an async function to send chart images to the external REST API. The function should accept a File or Blob parameter, construct a FormData object with the image in a field named 'imagem', POST to '/analisar-grafico' endpoint, and return the parsed JSON response. Include proper error handling for network failures and non-200 responses.

requirements.summary: Define TypeScript interfaces for external API response schema
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript interface ApiAnalysisResponse defines all required fields with correct types
Interface includes sinal as 'COMPRA' | 'VENDA' literal type
Interface includes tendencia, confianca, padroes array, and explicacao fields
Interface is exported and used for type safety in API service module

file_operations.description: Create a new types file that defines the ApiAnalysisResponse interface matching the external API response schema. Include fields: sinal (literal type 'COMPRA' | 'VENDA'), tendencia (string), confianca (number), padroes (string array), and explicacao (string). Export this interface for use in the API service and components.

requirements.summary: Update ProcessingScreen to call external API and handle responses
acceptanceCriteria: ProcessingScreen imports and calls the external API service function
Image data from route state is passed to API service
Processing stages animate during actual API request
API errors display user-friendly Portuguese messages
Success response navigates to Results page with API data
Loading states reflect real API request timing

file_operations.description: Import the analysisApi service and call it with the chart image from location state when the component mounts. Display the four processing stages ('Carregando imagem', 'Processando dados', 'Detectando padrões', 'Gerando resultado') while waiting for the API response. Handle API errors by showing Portuguese error messages with retry option. On success, transform the API response to match the AnalysisResult format and navigate to Results page with the data.

Create a utility function that maps the external API response structure (sinal, tendencia, confianca, padroes, explicacao) to the internal AnalysisResult type expected by the Results page. Handle the conversion of 'COMPRA'/'VENDA' signals to AnalysisDirection enum values, map padroes to CandlestickPattern enum values, and populate other required fields with appropriate defaults or derived values.

requirements.summary: Update Results page to render API response data
acceptanceCriteria: Results page accepts and displays API response data structure
Signal (sinal) displays as COMPRA or VENDA with appropriate directional arrows
Confidence (confianca) displays as percentage
Trend (tendencia) displays with existing Portuguese formatting
Patterns (padroes) list displays detected pattern names
Explanation (explicacao) displays as descriptive text
All existing Portuguese labels and styling are preserved

file_operations.description: Ensure the Results page correctly renders analysis data received from the external API after transformation. Verify that the mapped AnalysisResult structure displays correctly in all sections: signal direction with arrows, confidence percentage, trend description, detected patterns list, and textual explanation. Maintain all existing Portuguese labels and visual styling.

Update the SignalCard component if necessary to handle the API-sourced direction values and ensure proper display of COMPRA/VENDA signals with appropriate colors, icons, and Portuguese labels. Verify compatibility with the transformed API response data structure.

requirements.summary: Add multi-provider configuration support to API service
acceptanceCriteria: API service module includes configuration for multiple AI provider endpoints
Configuration supports OpenAI, Google Gemini, and custom Python backend URLs
Provider selection mechanism allows easy switching (e.g., via environment variable or config file)
Code structure is prepared to handle provider-specific request formatting if needed
Default configuration points to a placeholder endpoint that can be replaced

file_operations.description: Add a configuration object or environment variable mechanism to support multiple AI provider endpoints (OpenAI ChatGPT Vision, Google Gemini Vision, custom Python backend). Include placeholder URLs for each provider and implement provider selection logic (e.g., via VITE_AI_PROVIDER env var). Structure the code to allow provider-specific request formatting if different providers require different payload structures or headers.

Create a configuration file that defines provider endpoint URLs, default provider selection, and any provider-specific settings. Include constants for OpenAI, Google Gemini, and Python backend endpoints with placeholder values that can be overridden via environment variables. Export a function to get the active provider configuration.

requirements.summary: Add error handling and retry logic to API service
acceptanceCriteria: API service catches network errors and HTTP error responses
User-friendly Portuguese error messages display for common failure scenarios
Retry logic attempts failed requests up to a reasonable limit
Timeout handling prevents indefinite waiting
ProcessingScreen shows error state with option to retry or go back

file_operations.description: Implement comprehensive error handling for network failures, timeouts, and non-200 HTTP responses in the API service. Add retry logic (e.g., up to 2 retries with exponential backoff) for transient failures. Define Portuguese error messages for common scenarios: 'Erro ao conectar com o servidor' (connection error), 'Tempo esgotado' (timeout), 'Erro na análise' (analysis error). Return structured error objects that components can display to users.

Update ProcessingScreen to display error states from API failures with Portuguese error messages. Add a retry button that calls the API service again with the same image data. Include a back/cancel button to return to the AnalyzeChart page. Show appropriate loading states during retry attempts.
